item.title: Nordea First-Quarter Fee Growth Offsets Drop in Lending Income
item.description: Finnish lender Nordea Bank Abp reported profit from lending in the first quarter that declined largely in line with estimates, counterbalanced by profit generated from fees.

item.title: Segantii Wins Permission to Use BofA Documents for Legal Defense
item.description: Segantii Capital Management won a small victory ahead of its insider-trading trial, when a Hong Kong judge said the firm would be permitted to use key documents from Bank of America Corp. as part of its legal defense — something the Wall Street bank had tried to block.

item.title: Iran Tankers Go Dark to Sail Past US Blockade Laden With Crude
item.description: At least two fully laden Iranian tankers have sailed out of the Persian Gulf and past a US blockade this week, part of a flotilla that has made its way around the warships and ferried roughly 9 million barrels of oil to the market.

item.title: Crypto Billionaire Sues Trump-Linked Project Alleging Extortion
item.description: Crypto billionaire Justin Sun and his companies sued World Liberty Financial, a crypto project co-founded by President Donald Trump, accusing the company of extortion and an “illegal scheme” to seize his tokens.

item.title: Korea FX Deposits Slide by Record as Weak Won Spurs Conversions
item.description: South Korea’s foreign-currency deposits fell by a record in March as a weaker won prompted companies to convert holdings into local currency.

item.title: Will Hungary Join the Euro? It’s a Long Road After Orban
item.description: Hungary’s new leader has made adopting the euro one of his top priorities, a step that would mark the largest expansion of the single-currency bloc since Greece joined in 2001.

item.title: The TACO That Ate Market Strategy
item.description: Why ‘buy the dip’ is Trump Always Chickens Out’s best friend.

item.title: Three Bedrooms for €60 Million Shows Monaco’s Dirty Money Headache
item.description: A leaked trove of emails regarding the world’s priciest real estate offers a window into how the principality grapples with money laundering.

item.title: Singapore Says Trade Through Malacca Strait Must Remain Free
item.description: Singapore’s Foreign Minister Vivian Balakrishnan said passage through the straits of Malacca and Singapore, a key trade chokepoint brought into focus by the war in Iran, must remain free for all and that the city-state won’t support any efforts to restrict it.

item.title: China’s Sale of Yuan Bonds in Hong Kong Draws Record-Low Yield
item.description: China’s biggest yuan bond sale in Hong Kong since 2023 drew record-low yields for both two- and 15-year debt, as Beijing speeds up its push to internationalize the currency and mop up pockets of excess liquidity offshore.

item.title: Chinese Regulator Promotes Floating-Rate Bonds to Manage Risks
item.description: China’s interbank bond market regulator is pushing for an increase in the issuance of floating-rate notes to help manage risks from potential interest-rate volatility.

item.title: Cost Blowout Forces Australia to Slash Spending on Disabled
item.description: Australia’s center-left government is moving to rein in the ballooning costs of its signature disability welfare program as it tries to frame next month’s budget against the backdrop of a global energy crisis.

item.title: Traders Push Korea Leveraged Bets to Record as Chip Stocks Rally
item.description: Leveraged bets on South Korean equities jumped to a record as the return of the artificial intelligence trade prompts retail investors to chase a rally in tech heavyweights.

item.title: Vietnam’s GSM Taxi Plans to Hold IPO in 2028, Founder Says
item.description: Vietnam’s ride-hailing taxi Green & Smart Mobility JSC, which is owned by the country’s richest man, plans an initial public offering in the second half of 2028, according to founder Pham Nhat Vuong.

item.title: Small Caps Lead as India’s Stock-Market Rally Widens
item.description: Nifty 50’s breadth signals a new bull market, according to Macquarie.

item.title: China’s ‘National Team’ Cut ETF Stakes Below 20% Disclosure Mark
item.description: China’s “national team” has stepped back from its dominant role in the country’s biggest stock ETFs, pointing to efforts to rein in an overheated rally earlier this year.

item.title: Japan Finance Minister to Meet Banks to Discuss Mythos Threat
item.description: Japan’s Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama plans to meet the country’s biggest banks and other financial institutions to discuss Anthropic PBC’s latest AI model Mythos as early as this week, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Putin’s Oil Windfall Won’t Revive His Slowing Wartime Economy
item.description: The oil windfall triggered by the war in the Middle East is unlikely to help President Vladimir Putin revive Russia’s sluggish economy that’s teetering on the edge of recession.

item.title: RBA Is Monitoring Anthropic’s Mythos AI Over Cyberattack Fears
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia is closely monitoring developments around Anthropic PBC’s new Mythos AI model, which the company says is powerful enough to enable sophisticated cyberattacks.

item.title: China Exporters Beset by Yuan Surge Look to Sell Dollar Rallies
item.description: Gloria Yu’s company exports bicycles and related accessories to as far as Europe and Africa. Having suffered “heavy losses” on some orders due to the yuan’s rapid gains earlier in the year, Yu is looking to better manage her dollar exposure.

item.title: Treasury Wine Jumps Most in 12 Years on Strong China Results
item.description: Shares in Treasury Wine Estates Ltd. rose the most in 12 years after the Australian vintner announced a sharp jump in sales to retailers during the first months of 2026.

item.title: Blackstone’s AirTrunk Plans Its First Data Center-Backed Bond
item.description: Blackstone Inc.-owned data center operator AirTrunk Pte. is looking to raise at least A$500 million ($358 million) through asset-backed bonds, according to people familiar with the matter, in what may be one of Asia’s first in the sector.

item.title: Cochlear Plunges Most in 30 Years After Profit Guidance Cut
item.description: Cochlear Ltd. shares plummeted the most in more than 30 years after the Australian company cut its fiscal year profit guidance.

item.title: Crypto Whale Li Moves 20-Strong Trading Team to Tap Asia’s Rich
item.description: Crypto tycoon Li Lin is moving an entire trading team of about 20 people from his single-family office to Bitfire Group Holdings Ltd., a wealth manager in which he owns a 30% stake, to help the firm tap more private banking clients.

item.title: ASMPT Sees Second-Quarter Sales Forecast Beat Due to AI Demand
item.description: Chip gear maker ASMPT Ltd. sees its second-quarter revenue beating expectations, driven by strong growth from its semiconductor business critical for AI.

item.title: Indonesia Set to Hold Key Rate as Rupiah Hits Record Lows on Oil
item.description: Indonesia’s central bank will likely keep its benchmark interest rate unchanged to support the rupiah, which has hit a succession of record lows since the start of the US war on Iran.

item.title: Japan’s Export Growth Quickens as China Returns from Holidays
item.description: Japan’s exports grew at a faster clip in March as demand in China rebounded, while the impact from the Middle East conflict hasn’t yet had much impact on imports.

item.title: Hainan’s Duty-Free Rally Hinges on Wary Chinese Consumers
item.description: After two sluggish years, duty-free shopping is snapping back on China’s Hainan island, where domestic tourists are once again crowding malls for luxury handbags, gold jewelry and skincare.

item.title: Apple’s New CEO Will Need to Stave Off Exodus of Top Talent
item.description: John Ternus, Apple Inc.’s next chief executive officer, is up against a major challenge as he settles into the role: retaining top talent during a time of historic turnover.

item.title: UK Says Iran, China Drive Regular Significant Cyberattacks
item.description: Hackers linked to foreign governments are now behind the majority of “nationally significant” cyberattacks targeting the UK, the country’s top cybersecurity official is set to warn.
